reports.title: H7 M26 vs H6 S21 — GPS moving test + battery (in progress)
reports.summary: 14 Jun 2026: ~15 min drive at ~1 Hz GPS (11:30–11:45 NZ). M26 (H7) matched S21 fix rate with better accuracy (3.3 m vs 5.2 m median). Battery test 06:45–13:15 NZ: 96%→76% over 6.5 h (~3.1 %/h, ~32 h from full charge at 1 Hz).
notes: M26 field-validated Jun 2026: 3.3 m median GPS, ~3.1 %/h @ 1 Hz, ~32 h from full charge. PDF report updated with full session data.
M26 (H7) is the One NZ quote handset; H6 is Samsung Galaxy S21 reference.
8 h active regatta day at 1 Hz uses ~25% battery — no mid-day charge required.
Both devices production-ready at ~1 Hz for regatta tracking.

reports.title: A1 & A6 — long session battery (30 s GPS)
reports.summary: Both phones on continuous RNZ recorder sessions with ~30 s GPS. A1 started at 100%; A6 assumed 75% from prior test.
notes: Battery % is reported by the native app about every 10 minutes on GPS or heartbeat samples.
A6 drain is much lower than A1 on the same ingest path — useful as a low-drain reference device.
Plan ~1 full day on A1 for 30 s GPS + background recording; A6 substantially longer.

reports.title: A1 & A6 — short morning test (~2.5 h, 30 s GPS)
reports.summary: Morning battery check after ~6:30 AM NZ start with 30 s update interval configured.
notes: Consistent with the longer session: A1 ~3.7–4 %/h, A6 ~1.5–2 %/h.

reports.title: GPS moving test — 30 s interval (A1 & A6)
reports.summary: Active recording windows show ~30 s median GPS spacing; full-session averages are diluted by idle/doze periods.

charts.title: Median GPS interval — active vs full session (seconds)

notes: 30 s GPS setting is working whenever the app is actively logging.
Long gaps (~4 min p90 on full session) are stationary / screen-off / doze, not upload failures.
Optional faster uploads (~2.5 s) on the phone use more battery; dashboard smooth map does not require it.

reports.title: H6 dual-upload test — Recorder vs Traccar
reports.summary: Same ~22 km drive (~45 min) logged to RNZ Recorder (device H6) and Traccar (John Start) between 11:30–12:15 NZ time. Both tracks overlay on the map below; positions agree to within GPS noise. Fix spacing was ~1 Hz on both systems; Recorder had one 3.8 s gap in the whole session.
notes: Traccar device John Start (id 36) received the same phone upload path as Recorder H6 — not a separate Traccar hardware unit named H6.
Traccar speed is stored in knots; overlay maps convert to m/s (× 0.514).
Tracks are nearly identical (~1 Hz). Traccar had slightly more points and shorter max gaps; median position difference was 0 m.

Gap rarity (Recorder): 99.9% of intervals were normal ~1 s spacing. The 3.81 s max gap occurred once at 12:11:24 NZ; two other gaps were ~2.0 s (11:44:12 and 11:50:37). Traccar had no gaps above 1.5 s.

Live map: you would not see two different trails — Altitude HD shows one source at a time (Traccar or Recorder toggle). The rare Recorder gap would look like a brief stick-and-jump, not a constant lag.
Perceived live lag on overlay maps is dominated by browser poll interval (default 10 s, min 3 s in hub settings), not the ~0.5 s timestamp difference between backends.
RNZ Recorder monitor dashboard (H6) uses predict-to-now smoothing; Altitude HD live maps show the raw last fix for either source.
Closest approach to hub start pin (~186 m) at ~12:13 NZ — useful reference for geofence tuning.
Choose platform by workflow: Traccar for geofences and fleet maps; Recorder for RNZ Postgres history and device id H6.

reports.title: H6 — morning drive & overlay smoothing
reports.summary: Car test for yellow predict-to-now overlay vs raw green fix; rowing vs car speed caps on dashboard.
notes: Green dot = raw fix; yellow = predict-to-now (max 2.5 s, distance cap).
Car mode allows larger prediction offset at highway speed without rowing cap.

reports.title: Postgres storage & historical retention
reports.summary: RNZ ingest stores samples in Neon Postgres; monitor shows usage against POSTGRES_STORAGE_LIMIT_MB (default 512 MB).

metrics.label: Per-sample fields
metrics.value: GPS, motion, HR, stroke, capsize, tilt, battery, heartbeat

notes: See live storage chart on this page (refreshed from production).
Setup: docs/POSTGRES-SETUP.md in rowing_app repo.
Delete old data from monitor Settings → data management when quota is high.

reports.title: Data usage & ingest rates
reports.summary: Typical phone upload rates and server load for fleet monitoring at 30 s GPS.
notes: 270 devices @ 0.5 Hz ≈ 135 samples/s peak — see cost estimate notes in rowing_app docs.
AHD - LiveStream can switch source: Traccar (default) or Recorder (RNZ/KRI) in hub Settings.
